result.meeting_context: This is a Visual Arts Committee (VAC) meeting for the City of Mountain View discussing public art strategy development, capital improvement program updates, and planning for 2023 activities including artist calls and meeting schedules.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Susie Merrar contacted in advance and will have excused absence due to travel
points.source: John Lang : Susie did contact me in advance of the meeting. Let me know she is out travelling. This will be an excused absence.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: September 14th, 2222 meeting minutes approved unanimously by roll call vote
points.source: Jesse Cupp : All right, motion passes.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Three topics remain on agenda for November: public art strategy work, Bruce England discussion on placement landscaping integrations, and placemaking opportunities for pedestrian programs
points.source: John Lang : One is we'll bring back more work in the November agenda on public art strategy. We do have some of that conversation happening tonight. We'll have more of it at the November meeting.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee needs to update K5 policy by working backwards from real CIP projects to identify what language is needed for public art qualification
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I think what we need to do is kind of work backwards from real CIP projects and say, OK, here's a bike lane, a bike infrastructure project. What language do we need to include to make sure that it's public art because it didn't qualify?

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Castro Street pedestrianization presents tremendous opportunity to introduce public art and inject concept into public mindset with available funding and Downtown Committee interest
points.source: Clifford Bryant : I see that as a tremendous opportunity for us to introduce art into the public mindset, both with the money that's going to be available in that area with plus the interest of the Downtown Committee and revitalizing Castro.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: City Council approved resolutions to permanently close Castro Street as pedestrian mall, with final ordinance adoption scheduled for October 25th council meeting
points.source: John Lang : So last night City Council did approve resolutions to permanently close Castro Street. There's one more step to that, but effectively that is just adopting the ordinance at next week at an October 25th council meeting.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Council developed small interim CIP budget for necessary ADA access improvements and other elements for Castro Street
points.source: John Lang : In addition, council did develop ACIP budget, a small interim budget for some necessary improvements mainly for ADA access and some other elements as part of that.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: City Council established ad hoc committee for pedestrian mall focusing on design standards, guidelines, implementation plan including wayfinding and art incorporation with November meeting anticipated
points.source: John Lang : So City Council has developed an ad hoc committee for the pedestrian mall. It is a very short term Council committee subcommittee that is looking at the design standards, guidelines, implementation plan and their proposed next agenda is I think all the elements you talk about on wayfinding, incorporation of art.

topics.topic: Capital Improvement Program Art Project Updates

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Rangstorf Park Aquatic Center project is approximately 25% complete and on track to open late fall 2023 with artist coordinating with design team
points.source: John Lang : So on Rangstorf Park Aquatic Center, the project is approximately 25% complete and is on track actually to open late fall 2023. The we have been coordinating, the artist has been coordinating with the design team as part of that project.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Fayette Park construction just started with no significant updates beyond project being under construction
points.source: John Lang : The Fayette Park construction just started on the project. At this point, there's no significant update other than. The project is now under construction.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Rangsdorf park maintenance and tennis buildings design being wrapped up with bid expected before end of year, while staff works to resolve artist contract insurance impediments
points.source: John Lang : Rangsdorf park maintenance and tennis buildings design is being wrapped up and they do expect that bid to go out before end of this year. And as your staff, I continue to work with the artists to get their contracts resolved working through some insurance impediments still

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Pyramid Park had dedication ceremony in September which committee members attended
points.source: John Lang : And then Pyramid Park did have its dedication in September, which I know a few members were able to attend.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Pyramid Park exceeded expectations in size with beautifully designed children's playground featuring metal canopy casting shaded light
points.source: Clifford Bryant : One, the park is immense. I was really surprised because we've seen these little things, little bitty things like Amora Park etcetera kind of been shoehorned in while the the Pyramid Park is is just I I was blown away with how the size of it and also the beauty of the children's playground I thought was beautifully done with the canopy, the metal canopy casting kind of a shaded light on on the ground, just beautifully done.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Mobile sculpture was less colorful than expected and got lost visually due to shininess blending with basketball court poles in bright light
points.source: Clifford Bryant : I was a little bit disappointed with the public art, specifically the the mobile I for some reason I thought it was going to be more colourful than it turned out to be and that may have just been stuck in my mind and maybe miss misrepresenting it or miss not remembering correctly. But when I saw it and then saw where it was placed with the basketball courts, suddenly the the shininess of the basketball poles and the shininess of the sculpture caused it all kind of to me, the sculpture to get lost.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Bee and butterfly sculpture placement resulted in bees being visible only from 5 feet of park due to positioning at edge near row houses, limiting visibility
points.source: Jesse Cupp : The way it was positioned, the bees are visible for like 5 feet of the park. You have to stand behind the sculpture. It's like right at the edge of the park. So it's basically just visible to people who live in those houses back there.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee proposes moving artist selection process from beginning to after park construction completion to better understand physical environment and improve art placement decisions
points.source: Clifford Bryant : So my thought was what would should we think about moving the process not at the beginning, but at the end. Once the park is defined, we kind of know what it's going to be like. We know what the beautiful play equipment is going to be like. It's been selected and that we now then and introduce into that environment pieces that would complement it.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Strategy follows funnel approach starting with vision statement: creation of diverse public art enriches Mountain View's innovative and creative culture, enhances vibrancy, supports local artists, transforms Mountain View into arts hub
points.source: John Lang : Where it landed at the time was the vision for this strategy is the creation of divide diverse public art and reaches. Mountain View's innovative and creative culture enhances the vibrancy of our community, supports local artists, artists and transforms Mountain View into an arts hub.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Four established goals include building community, placemaking, reflecting heritage, and economic development through art
points.source: John Lang : The public art goals were identified fairly early on as an exercise with Barbara Goldstein. Some of you may remember that the goals were, you know, how do we build community through art Place create place making through art. Can art better reflect Mountain View's heritage And then how can art achieve economic development goals.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Three strategic approaches identified: develop/augment/refine city policies, create more public art opportunities through partnerships, pursue new collaborations with artists and arts organizations
points.source: John Lang : So one strategy that is going to be deployed is to develop or augment or even refine city policies. So what do I mean by this? Develop a fee for private art. That's a new policy. So develop a new policy. Augment some existing policy, maybe the city's donation policy. Be more reflective of arts, or refine the K5 policy, which was noted earlier.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Strategy timeframe should be five years with specific measurable goals and review process, as longer timeframes change language and goal specificity
points.source: Toni Hsu : Are we, are we thinking this is a five year strategy or 10 year strategy because for me that depending on that timeline it changes to your point, it changes kind of the goals and like the language, right. Because for example for five years I would want to see I think some of those initiatives be a little more specific and pointed since we our goal is five years

points.speaker: Don Whitebread
points.text: Mountain View needs more venues for local artists to show work, as many have space and materials but lack exhibition opportunities, with most commissioned artists coming from outside Mountain View
points.source: Don Whitebread : I think if we really want to encourage the grander artistic future of Mountain View, I don't know what it looks like, but it would be nice if we provided more venues for the people that are creating art along with, you know, putting up big art. You know the the art that we're commissioning is from people at the the top of the art pyramid. The real professionals, and that's most of them, aren't from Mountain View.

points.speaker: Regina Sakols
points.text: City needs affordable studio spaces for artists, noting other cities like Palo Alto, Belmont, Redwood City, and San Carlos have established studio space programs
points.source: Regina Sakols : Well, I think I agree with that and but I also think that we need studio spaces that are, you know, affordable.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee should think beyond traditional gallery spaces and explore innovative exhibition methods including internet-based or temporary outdoor installations to serve more artists
points.source: Toni Hsu : What if we think differently and maybe there is a way or mechanism where we can't, where 1000 artists can show their art, right? Like, again, I'm making this up, but maybe it's somehow through the Internet.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee voted unanimously to adopt resolution authorizing virtual meetings pursuant to AB361 to maintain flexibility if City Council returns to in-person meetings
points.source: Jesse Cupp : Yes, all right. Motion passes

topics.topic: Center for Performing Arts Artist Call

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Annual CPA artist call returns after COVID hiatus, seeking 6-8 artists for year-long exhibitions with 6-8 week individual displays, requiring resume and 10 art piece examples
points.source: John Lang : So every year there historically has been a call for artists and this is for artists to exhibit in the CPA for a year. So artists will exhibit anywhere from 6 to 8 weeks in the CPA.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Environmental theme had highest participation rate while disability theme had low participation with most artists not submitting theme-related work
points.source: Jesse Cupp : In my opinion, I think the environmental team won probably had the most highest rate of participation from the artists home and the global community one. Those were pretty good and the disability one really good intentions, but I think it kind of bombed to be honest because most artists just didn't submit work related to the theme.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee decided on no theme for 2023 call to attract maximum artist participation and establish CPA as exhibition venue before adding thematic constraints
points.source: Toni Hsu : Yeah, I, I, I, I, I prefer not to have a theme and I I think 1. The one I was here for the accessibility one and we did not get a lot of, I don't know how many we picked. We picked like 10 but there were 12 total submissions, right. It was really low and a theme really limits us.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee unanimously adopted 2023 meeting schedule for second Wednesday of nine months at 6:00 PM, excluding January, July, and August
points.source: Jesse Cupp : All right. Thank you.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: John Lang thanked committee members for their good work and expressed appreciation for public arts strategy feedback and monthly contributions
points.source: John Lang : I'll certainly follow up on the ad hoc committee as well discussion and just wanted to express my appreciation and thanks. I'm sorry I was not at the celebration of service, but really appreciate all the good work you do on a monthly basis and I've enjoyed this conversation on the public arts right? And really appreciate the feedback this evening.

decisions.text: Committee will develop series of recommendations at next meeting to present to Castro Street ad hoc committee representing VAC's official position
decisions.source: Clifford Bryant : The way so would it then be good for us at our next meeting to come up with a series of recommendations that we would vote on to present to that group? So it kind of gives off official should we say official stamp from us and then have Jesse kind of follow up when they meet to kind of represent, you know to have a face there to to to to to represent us with that

decisions.text: John Lang will share Castro Street ad hoc committee agenda and first meeting details to provide context for VAC recommendations
decisions.source: John Lang : Yes, I can certainly work to get that agenda. As for that conversation to have, I would also be happy to share after this meeting basically what was talked about at the first meeting.

decisions.text: John Lang will issue CPA call for artists in late October with no theme, targeting Bay Area geographic range and including public art strategy information
decisions.source: John Lang : So it sounds like there's some consensus about no theme this year. Just do open call, we'll put out the parameters as we've done in the in the past
